mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in real retail activity signals near-term revenue growth for listed firms; higher RSAFS growth ranks predict positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns through stronger expected sales and earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Reject if the rolling 3-year walk-forward Rank IC between RSAFS 3-month growth and 20-day S&P 500 returns is below 0.03, or if top-quintile RSAFS growth does not outperform bottom-quintile growth.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating household income and recession risk; when UNRATE crosses upward by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, S&P 500 20-day forward returns turn negative.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses upward by > 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if average triggered returns are not below unconditional returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid policy tightening raises discount rates and reduces risk appetite; when FEDFUNDS crosses upward by more than 50 basis points over 3 months, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses upward by > 0.50pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns after tightening triggers exceed the unconditional mean return, or if the triggered negative-hit rate is below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth paired with falling inflation identifies a productivity-friendly expansion; when INDPRO rises over 3 months and CPIAUCSL inflation decelerates, margins and valuation multiples support positive 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change > 0 and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation change < 0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO-up and CPIAUCSL-decelerating state does not outperform either single-series signal, or if its 20-day mean excess return is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts leads construction, durable-goods demand, and credit creation; when HOUST crosses above its 6-month average by more than 10%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above its 6-month average by > 10%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing-strength episodes have a 60-day average return below the unconditional average, or if positive-return frequency is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains combined with falling unemployment identify broad labor-income recovery; when PAYEMS rises over 3 months and UNRATE falls over 3 months, S&P 500 20-day returns are positive through earnings momentum.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-up and unemployment-down state has no positive excess return versus unconditional 20-day returns, or if its Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration while policy rates remain pinned compresses expected real rates but raises future tightening risk; when CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized inflation exceeds 4% and FEDFUNDS is at or below 0.25%, 20-day returns are negative.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized inflation crosses above 4% while FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered zero-rate inflation episodes produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or if returns are not lower than other zero-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals supply disruption and lower operating leverage; when INDPRO crosses downward by more than 2% over 3 months, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses downward by > 2% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day forward returns following INDPRO contraction triggers are positive in more than 55% of cases, or if triggered mean returns are not below unconditional 5-day returns.
